Figure 1:Evaluation accuracy (avg@16, averaged over AIME24 and AIME25) over
training atδ=0.15\delta=0.15on the four settings: Qwen3-4B-Base, Qwen3-8B-Base, Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base with
FP8 E2E training, and Qwen3-30B-A3B-Base with FP8 Rollout. The two predictive divergence masks use the aggregated-tail and uniform-tail
estimators for the top-KKKL directional-derivative coefficient.
Figure 2:Evaluation accuracy (avg@16, averaged over AIME24 and AIME25) across the four settings with divergence thresholdδ=0.05\delta=0.05.

Figure 3:Unsafe-keep rate on disagreement tokens(δ=0.15\delta=0.15,6161seeds). We consider tokens outside the trust region
(D>δD>\delta) on which the ratio-based and divergence-based direction criteria
make opposite keep/clip decisions. For each criterion, among the disagreement
tokens it keeps, we report the fraction whose divergence increases after the
update (Δ​D>0\Delta D>0). Lower is better.(a)Mean unsafe-keep rate
across seeds. The divergence-based direction criterion is lower than the
ratio-based criterion (34.2%34.2\%vs.36.9%36.9\%). Error bars show standard
errors.(b)Per-seed paired comparison, with each line corresponding
to one seed and colored by which criterion has the lower unsafe-keep rate.

Figure 4:Realized divergence change of kept disagreement tokens(δ=0.15\delta=0.15,6161seeds). For each direction criterion, we collect the
disagreement tokens it keeps and measureΔ​D=Dpost−Dpre\Delta D=D_{\text{post}}-D_{\text{pre}}after one actual update. Tokens from all
seeds are combined in this figure.(a)Empirical CDF ofΔ​D\Delta D.
The shaded regionΔ​D<0\Delta D<0indicates kept updates that contracted the
divergence.(b)Fraction of kept updates withΔ​D<0\Delta D<0. The
divergence-based direction criterion keeps a larger fraction of contracting
updates than the ratio-based criterion (65.1%65.1\%vs.62.9%62.9\%).
